openFindings.note: Packet docs and changelog still describe the shared_space_id cleanup as one-time / first-startup, but the runtime retries the DROP COLUMN attempt on every bootstrap while the column still exists on unsupported SQLite builds.
memory/save.md, memory/manage.md, and memory/README.txt still describe a legacy memory/ continuity surface even though the shipped contract retires standalone memory/*.md artifacts.

The workflow YAML family (deep-review, deep-research, spec_kit_complete, plus the plan/implement save-context flows) still describes indexing generated support artifacts and authoring guardrails under memory/ instead of pinning continuity to canonical spec documents.

The live feature catalog for memory_index_scan still says the scanner discovers retired spec-folder memory files even though memory-index-discovery.ts excludes memory/ directories and the runtime keeps packet continuity spec-doc-first.

resolvedFindings.note: Current runtime save/index entry points no longer accept or discover retired specs/**/memory/*.md public paths.
The current context-server harness mocks discoverMemoryFiles and no longer carries the removed findMemoryFiles surface.
Lifecycle playbooks 097 and 144 now reference canonical spec docs (decision-record.md and implementation-summary.md) instead of retired memory artifacts.

repeatedFindings.note: Exact-pattern closure sweep over packet docs, changelog, workflow YAMLs, manuals, and key runtime files found no hidden P0/P1 beyond the stable residual F005/F006/F007 cluster.
Targeted correctness regression probe re-validated the spec-doc-health walk-up and context-server mock cleanup without reopening retired memory-path acceptance or stale test references.

Adversarial live-entry-point re-read confirmed that memory_save, memory_index_scan, create-agent, and representative completion workflows do not reopen retired spec-folder memory/*.md acceptance or emission.
Terminal convergence pass confirmed that the residual set remains exactly four documentary P1 findings and that no reviewed runtime or security surface reopened.
